description_long: This bungalow features a traditional wooden exterior with a prominent thatched or shingled gable roof supported by visible rafters. The front facade includes a raised porch area with wooden posts and a sliding shoji-style door, alongside a small window with wooden framing. The side walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, and the rear of the structure mirrors the front with additional sliding doors and porch space. The roof extends over the porch, providing shelter and architectural interest. The overall design is compact, single-story, and evokes a rustic, vernacular style reminiscent of Japanese or Southeast Asian rural homes.
description: A single-story wooden bungalow with a gable roof, raised porch, sliding shoji doors, and a rustic, traditional appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a sliding shoji door, a small window, and a covered porch area.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the horizontal wooden plank siding and the full length of the gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with a wide sliding door and porch, similar to the front but without the small window.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the wooden siding and the depth of the roof structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with front and side windows
